Discover How Torah Speaks to You

  • Begin with the Torah text
  • Respond to thought-provoking questions

  • Learn from respected resources
  • Uncover lessons that speak to your own life

For every faith. Every background. Every curious mind.

Explore the Book

Dear Reader,

I didn't grow up studying Torah. In fact, I came to it later in life and often felt disconnected from the text. It seemed ancient, distant, and sometimes hard to relate to.

But I was curious. I wanted to understand. So I began to study—one Torah portion at a time, asking a simple but life-changing question:

What is God trying to teach us through these stories?

That one question opened a door. Torah came alive. The stories spoke to me—about relationships, choices, challenges, and the timeless values that guide meaningful living.

For nine years, I shared these insights in a weekly study group that I created and facilitated, asked hard questions, and discovered how relevant Torah truly is—for today, for all of us.

Over fourteen years of study and reflection, supported by hundreds of wise teachers from many traditions, this book was born.

It is not meant to tell you what to think. It is an invitation to think, feel, question, and discover for yourself.

When we study Torah with an open heart, it becomes a conversation between the past and our present, between ancient wisdom and our everyday lives.

My deepest hope is that the questions, stories, and insights in these pages spark your own journey—just as they ignited mine.

Welcome. I'm so glad you're here.

Dr. Judy Freier The Torah Lady

Published Work

The Book

View Full Screen

Now Available

Talking Torah for Today:
36 Life Lessons from the Book of Genesis

Dr. Judy Freier brings the ancient stories of Genesis to vivid life — showing how the struggles, choices, and wisdom of our ancestors speak directly to the challenges we face today. Each of the 36 lessons is warm, practical, and instantly applicable. This is Torah not as religious obligation, but as genuine guidance for living well.

Order on Amazon Request a signed copy →

"The Torah doesn't ask us to be perfect. It asks us to be present — to show up, wonder, argue, and keep returning."

Dr. Judy Freier · The Torah Lady

Parasha · Bereshit

Why "In the Beginning" Is Actually About Right Now

Parasha · Lech Lecha

What Abram Teaches Us About Leaving What Feels Safe

Reflection

The Gift of Struggle: Wrestling with the Angel as Metaphor
